stem: A GPS navigation navigation system uses 3 parallel satellite links for redundancy. The position fixed requires at least one satellite link to function. satellite link 1 has a reliability between 70% and 82% (uncertain due to limited operational data). satellite link 2 has a reliability of 93%. satellite link 3 has a reliability of 86%. What is the overall probability that the position fixed?

ground_truth.answer: P(position fixed) ranges from 0.9971 to 0.9982. Point estimate: 0.9976.

solution_steps: P(system works) = 1 - P(all 3 satellite links fail simultaneously).
At lower bound: P(all fail) = 0.3 × 0.07 × 0.14 = 0.0029. P(works) = 0.9971.
At upper bound: P(all fail) = 0.18 × 0.07 × 0.14 = 0.0018. P(works) = 0.9982.
common_failure_modes: Multiplying individual reliabilities instead of computing 1 - P(all fail)
Using a single reliability for the uncertain component without propagating uncertainty
Treating the system as a series (all must work) rather than parallel (any one suffices)
